title: Vurrulf
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image= |name=Vurrulf |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]]''[[I, Jedi]]'' |gender= |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|Combined Defense Forces of the New Republic]]''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' ***[[New Republic Starfighter Command/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Command]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]'' confirms that [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] operates within [[New Republic Starfighter Command/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Command]]. ****[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|This is Nine on one. Someone watch my back.''"
"''I copy, on it.|Corran Horn and Vurrulf|}} '''Vurrulf''' was a [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] elite [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. While the Rogues were [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] the [[Invids|Invid]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], Vurrulf served as a member of the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron's]] [[Three Flight]]. [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] learned that the Invids would be attacking a [[Glitterstar|luxury liner]] near the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Alakatha/Legends|Alakatha]], and Rogue Squadron laid in wait in-[[K'vath system|system]] before springing their [[Battle of Alakatha|trap]] on a group of Invid [[Ugly/Legends|ugly]] starfighters. Vurrulf came out of the [[Starfighter combat/Legends|battle]] unscathed, and the victorious Rogues returned to the New Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Pick targets carefully, three flight.|Corran Horn, to Vurrulf and the rest of Three Flight|}} [[File:Rogue_Squadron.svg|thumb|left|200px|Vurrulf flew as part of Rogue Squadron (emblem pictured).]] Vurrulf, a [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]], was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]].''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[I, Jedi]]'' to [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]]. The Rogues were a twelve-person [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighter]] pilots who served [[New Republic Starfighter Command/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Command]]. Vurrulf flew in the squadron's [[Three Flight]] along with [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate]] [[Ghufran]], as well as [[Rogue Ten/Legends|Rogue Ten]], [[Ooryl Qrygg]]; and [[Rogue Nine/Legends|Rogue Nine]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]]—the [[Flight group/Legends|flight]] group's [[Flight leader/Legends|leader]]. The Rogues were commanded overall by [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]], [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]]. 11 ABY saw Rogue Squadron assigned to [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]] for the elusive [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] group known as the [[Invids]], who managed to elude every New Republic attempt to capture them, leaving the Rogues with about a [[Standard week/Legends|week]] of down [[Time/Legends|time]] in between raids. When [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] received a tip that the Invids would be attacking the [[Luxury liner/Legends|luxury liner]] ''[[Glitterstar]]'' near the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Alakatha/Legends|Alakatha]], the Rogues were ferried almost all of the way to [[K'vath system|Alakatha's star system]] aboard the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']]. They finally entered the [[Star system/Legends|system]] using [[Precision hyperspace jump|micro-jumps]], and their X-wings went to ground on several large [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] near the moon. When the modified [[Bulk cruiser/Legends|bulk cruiser]] ''[[Booty Full]]'' arrived in-system and dispatched numerous [[Ugly/Legends|ugly]] starfighters to attack the ''Glitterstar'', Rogue Squadron intercepted and [[Battle of Alakatha|engaged]] them. Horn told Three Flight to pick their targets carefully, and after their enemies' ranks had been thinned considerably, Vurrulf covered Horn as the captain pursued and destroyed an ugly [[Clutch (fighter)|tri-fighter]]. The ''Booty Full'' quickly surrendered, and with all of the uglies defeated, ''Home One'' and two New Republic [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] entered the system to collect both the Rogues and the defeated bulk [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]]. Vurrulf and the Rogues underwent a debriefing aboard ''Home One'' while en route to the New Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and upon arrival, they all landed their X-wings in a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]]. Not long afterward, Vurrulf's flight leader, Corran Horn, left the squadron to train as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[New Jedi Order]]. ==Personality and traits== Vurrulf served in Rogue Squadron, an elite team of pilots that included the best in the entire [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]]. The Klatooinian survived at least one [[Starfighter combat/Legends|encounter]] with the Invid pirates, coming out of the engagement unscathed. ==Behind the scenes== Vurrulf appeared in [[Michael A. Stackpole]]'s [[1998]] novel ''[[I, Jedi]]''. ==Appearances== *''[[I, Jedi]]'' {{Ab|I, Jedi (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with unspecified pronouns]] [[Category:Klatooinians]] [[Category:Rogue Squadron personnel]]
